item.title: You darkness, from which I come
item.description: Rainer Maria Rilke You darkness, from which I come Jij duisternis, waaruit ik stam Du Dunkelheit, aus der ich stamme English · Nederlands · Original English You darkness, from which I come, I love you more than the flame that bounds the world, by shining for some circle, beyond which no being knows...

item.title: Without a why
item.description: Angelus Silesius Without a why Zonder waarom Ohne Warum English · Nederlands · Original English The rose is without a why.

item.title: For He may well be loved, but not thought
item.description: Anonymous For He may well be loved, but not thought Want Hij kan wel bemind worden, maar niet gedacht For whi He may wel be loved, bot not thought English · Nederlands · Original English But now you ask me and say: “How shall I think of Him Himself, and what is He?” And to this I can only answer yo...

item.title: Not Seen by Form or Sound
item.description: Anonymous Not Seen by Form or Sound Niet gezien door vorm of geluid Not Seen by Form or Sound English · Nederlands · Original English What do you think, Subhūti? Is the Tathāgata to be seen by the thirty-two marks? Subhūti said: No, Blessed One.

item.title: The Silence of Vimalakīrti
item.description: Anonymous The Silence of Vimalakīrti De stilte van Vimalakīrti The Silence of Vimalakīrti English · Nederlands · Original English After the bodhisattvas had each given their explanation, they asked Mañjuśrī: What is the bodhisattva’s entrance into the Dharma-door of nonduality? Mañjuśrī said: As I ...

item.title: What Speech Cannot Reveal
item.description: Anonymous What Speech Cannot Reveal Wat niet door spraak wordt uitgesproken What Speech Cannot Reveal English · Nederlands · Original English There the eye does not reach, nor speech, nor the mind.
